title: Priority topics
text:
Currently, the EFSPI methods leaders are working on five priority topics. These topics are aligned with both EFSPI and EFPIA and input from further stakeholders, e.g. the EFSPI / PSI SIGs, is sought as well. The idea is to contribute to scientific discussions through publications and presentations and to be prepared for discussions with regulators once there are opportunities, e.g. within EMA multistakeholder workshops.











Priority topic
Status
Lead(s)
Download charter




Application of Bayesian methods in drug development
Active
Mike Krams, Nicky Best
X


Covariate adjustment
Active
Alex Ocampo
X


A framework to appraise statistical methods building on ICH M15
Completed

X


Specific topics related to estimands
To be confirmed

AI

title: Newsletter 12th June 2026
text:
In April, the EFSPI statistical methodology leaders met for their yearly face-to-face meeting in Copenhagen. The meeting was co-organized by Novo Nordisk and Lundbeck. Ten members took two days out of their busy schedule to travel to Denmark. These topics were discussed.



The EML have met with EMA statisticians on 19th May 2026 and discussed the following topics:

Open question to regulators: What gaps do you observe that we could help improve?
Covariate adjustment.
The minutes of this meeting are available here.




See events page.

title: Contribution to EFSPI newsletter March 2025
text:
Yearly face-to-face meeting
In January, the EFSPI statistical methodology leaders met for their yearly face-to-face meeting at Merck in Darmstadt. Twelve members took two days out of their busy schedule to travel to Germany. Below we summarize the discussions.


Achievements
While this group is only in effect for a bit more than one year achievements are manifold:

presentations and industry at two EMA multistakeholder workshops on behalf EFPIA,
building a network of methodology leaders within Europe,
fostering thinking about invention, innovation, adoption and impact through roundtables, webinars, podcasts, etc.



Determination of priority topics
Four priority topics were identified for which dedicated subteams are having internal discussions and work on list of questions. See here.


Training offers
Building on the first webinar From Statistical Innovations to Common Practice: How to innovate with impact? the group plans to continue a webinar series sharing examples of successful commercializations of statistical inventions.


Career development for methodologists
Different models were discussed and we plan to have a discussion with the EFSPI statistical leaders how we can best collaborate to continue to build a network of methodologists.


Interaction with statistical engineering colleagues
The group will seek alignment with openstatsware on implementation of software for the four priority topics and will explore further opportunities for collaboration between the two groups.


Library of commercialisations
A library of successful commercialization examples of statistical inventions has been initiated. These examples will be worked up so that they can be shared with the community to help discussions on methods within companies, e.g. to get partner functions on board for certain statistical methods, not the least in a regulatory context.

title: EFSPI methodology leaders: achievements 2025
text:
Three presentations at EFSPI regulatory statistics workshop:

EFSPI method leaders (Mouna) presented the four priority topics.
Tobias presented on credibility framework/assumptions.
Nicky presented in the Bayes session.
See here for recordings and slidedecks.

EFSPI statistics leaders meeting: Kaspar and Mouna presented on the four priority topics.
Organization of BBS / EFSPI webinar From Statistical Innovations to Common Practice, How to innovate with impact?.
Collation of comments on draft ICH E20 and submission via EFSPI.
Contribution to EMA’s External Control workshop, see events.
Contribution to EMA’s Bayes Workshop, see events. Increased collaboration + synergies with EFPIA Clinical Trial Innovation Pillar, EFSPI Regulatory eSIG, contribution to yearly informal discussion with MHRA, jointly with Regulatory eSIG.
Community benefit: taking ideas back to our own companies/networks.
Companies align their priorities to the ones declared by this group.

title: 2026/05/12: Meeting EFSPI methods leaders - EMA statisticians
text:
2026/05/12: Meeting EFSPI methods leaders - EMA statisticians

Minutes.



2026/05/12: EFSPI StatsLeaders Meeting Munich

Mouna and Tobias lead a session on Engagement with Regulatory Agencies and Methodological Innovation.



2026/04/15: EFSPI Statistical Methodology Leaders F2F in Copenhagen
Topics that were discussed:

Update from organizations.
Change management tactics.
Priority topics, see here.



2025/11/03: EMA workshop on external controls

Mouna and Marc represented the group in the scientific committee while Natalia presented.
Recording and slidedecks.



2025/09/10: EFSPI regulatory statistics workshop

EFSPI method leaders (Mouna) presented the four priority topics.
Tobias presented on credibility framework/assumptions.
Nicky presented in the Bayes session.
See here for recordings and slidedecks.



2025/05/13: EFSPI StatsLeaders Meeting in Copenhagen

Mouna and Kaspar ran a session “Innovative clinical trial methodologies and their adoption - Priorities and Opportunities”.



2025/03/19: BBS webinar “From Statistical Innovations to Common Practice, How to innovate with impact?”
Slides and recording


2024/11/13: Regular meeting of EFSPI / PSI regulatory eSIG with MHRA statisticians
EFSPI methods leaders also provided input into the agenda of this meeting, and some also attended. Below the protocol of the discussion.

Summary of discussions.



2024/07/06: EMA multistakeholder workshop on general topics
EMA invited industry to propose topics for discussion, of which ten were selected for discussion, see the agenda below. Most short talks were given by members of the EFSPI statistical methodology leaders group.

Agenda.
All slidedecks.
Summary of discussions.



2024/06/17: EMA multistakeholder workshop on Bayesian statistics

Agenda.



2024/05/06: EFSPI StatsLeaders Meeting at BMS in Boudry

Mouna and Kaspar introduced the group.

For feedback and questions send an email to efspieurope@gmail.com.
